description_long: A group of three tall coconut palm trees is depicted, each with slender, slightly curved brown trunks and a crown of long, arching green fronds. The fronds are feathery and radiate outward, with some overlapping and creating a dense canopy at the top. The trunks show subtle textural markings and natural bends, giving a realistic appearance of mature coconut palms. At the top of the tallest trunk, a cluster of coconuts is visible, nestled among the fronds. The coloration transitions from deep brown at the base of the trunks to lighter, yellow-green hues in the leaves. The overall arrangement suggests a natural cluster, typical of tropical or subtropical urban landscaping.
description: Three mature coconut palm trees with arching green fronds and brown, textured trunks, featuring a visible cluster of coconuts at the crown.
description_view_0: Three coconut palms with overlapping fronds, two trunks leaning left and one upright, coconuts partially visible at the top.
description_view_1: Frontal view of the palms, with the tallest trunk and coconuts most prominent, fronds densely layered in the foreground.
description_view_2: All three trunks clearly visible, with fronds radiating outward and coconuts at the top center, showing the cluster arrangement.
description_view_3: Tallest palm trunk in the center, coconuts visible at the crown, fronds forming a dense canopy with less overlap than other views.
attribute_husk_pattern: The coconut husks visible in the cluster at the crown display a rough, fibrous texture with longitudinal striations. The fibers are densely packed, running mostly parallel to the axis of the coconut, with some irregularities and slight color variations from brown to tan.
attribute_stem_attachment: A visible stem attaches the coconuts to the palm, appearing as a short, thick stalk at the top center of the cluster, connecting the fruits to the main trunk among the fronds.
attribute_growth_cluster: Part of a cluster; three coconuts are visible, grouped closely together at the top of the tallest palm.
attribute_proximity_to_palm_trees: The coconuts are directly attached to the crown of the tallest palm tree, nestled among the fronds and in close proximity to the trunk.
attribute_urban_placement: Indeterminate; the background is plain white with no visible contextual elements to indicate urban, natural, or other environments.
